title: Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. What eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu praises…
content: Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. What eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu praises those who are not liked, criticizes those who are liked, desires gain, desires honor, has no moral shame, has no moral dread, has evil desires, and has wrong view. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is loved, liked, respected, and emulated by his fellow celibates. And, bhikkhus, what eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not praise those who are not liked, does not criticize those who are liked, does not desire gain, does not desire honor, has moral shame, has moral dread, has few desires, and has right view. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is loved, liked, respected, and emulated by his fellow celibates.

title: Here, Mahānāma, a noble disciple is learned, remembers what he has learned, and stores up what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in…
content: Here, Mahānāma, a noble disciple is learned, remembers what he has learned, and stores up what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, good in the end, with full meaning and phrasing, proclaiming the holy life completely perfect and pure — such teachings he has learned much, retained, recited verbally, mentally investigated, and well penetrated by right view.

title: The Blessed One: Abandoning the five strands of sensual desire, pleasant and agreeable, With faith, go forth, make an end of suffering. Associate with good…
content:
The Blessed One:
Abandoning the five strands of sensual desire, pleasant and agreeable,
With faith, go forth, make an end of suffering.

Associate with good friends, dwell in a remote lodging,
Secluded, quiet. Moderate in food,
Robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines —
Do not have craving for these things; do not return to the world.

Restrained in the Pātimokkha, guarded in the five senses,
Practice mindfulness of the body, live full of disenchantment.

Avoid the sign of the beautiful connected with passion;
Develop a mind unattached to the foul, concentrated, one-pointed.

title: Bhikkhus, there are these three perfections. What three? Perfection in virtue, perfection in mind, perfection in view. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in virtue? Here,…
content: Bhikkhus, there are these three perfections. What three? Perfection in virtue, perfection in mind, perfection in view. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in virtue? Here, bhikkhus, a person abstains from killing living beings, abstains from taking what is not given, abstains from sexual misconduct, abstains from false speech, abstains from divisive speech, abstains from harsh speech, abstains from idle chatter. This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in virtue. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in mind? Here, bhikkhus, a person is not covetous and has a mind without ill-will. This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in mind. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in view? Here, bhikkhus, a person holds right view, an undistorted view: 'There is giving, sacrifice, offering; there is fruit and result of good and bad actions; there is this world and the next world; there is mother, father; there are spontaneously reborn beings; there are samaṇas and brahmins in the world who, having practiced rightly, have realized and declared this world and the next by their own direct knowledge.' This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in view. Due to perfection in virtue ... due to perfection in mind ... due to perfection in view, bhikkhus, beings, after death, with the breakup of the body, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world. These, bhikkhus, are the three perfections.

title: Elder Sārabhaṅga: Breaking reeds with my hands, I made my own hut. Therefore, my common name was 'Reed-breaker'. Now it is no longer suitable to…
content:
Elder Sārabhaṅga:

Breaking reeds with my hands, I made my own hut.
Therefore, my common name was 'Reed-breaker'.

Now it is no longer suitable to break reeds with my own hands,
Following the teaching of the one named Gotama.

Previously, Sārabhaṅga had never seen the entire disease completely and fully.
Now that disease is seen through the word of the supreme one.

Without craving, without clinging, the seven Buddhas attained Nibbāna.
This Dhamma is taught by those who have realized the Dhamma.

Out of compassion for beings, the four truths are taught:
Suffering, the origin of suffering, the path,
Cessation, the end of suffering.

In continuous existence, suffering rolls on without end.
When this body is destroyed, when life ends,
There is no other rebirth. I am truly well liberated,
Liberated from all bases.

title: And, friends, what is yearning for the future? 'My eye in the future will be such and such, forms will be such and such, and…
content: And, friends, what is yearning for the future? 'My eye in the future will be such and such, forms will be such and such, and he directs his mind to obtain what is not yet obtained.' Because of that directing of mind, he delights in that. Delighting in that, he yearns for the future. 'My ear in the future will be such and such, sounds will be such and such... My nose in the future will be such and such, odors will be such and such... My tongue in the future will be such and such, flavors will be such and such... My body in the future will be such and such, tangibles will be such and such... My mind in the future will be such and such, mental objects will be such and such, and he directs his mind to obtain what is not yet obtained.' Because of that directing of mind, he delights in that. Delighting in that, he yearns for the future. Thus, friends, is yearning for the future.

title: Having known all abodes, not longing for any, That sage is truly a sage, without greed, without clinging, No longer striving, having reached the far…
content:
Having known all abodes, not longing for any,
That sage is truly a sage, without greed, without clinging,
No longer striving, having reached the far shore.

All-conquering, all-knowing, of perfect wisdom,
Undefiled regarding all things, abandoning all,
With craving destroyed, liberated —
The wise know that one as a sage.

Endowed with the power of wisdom, virtuous, observant,
Calm, delighting in jhāna, mindful,
Freed from all bonds, without taints —
The wise know that one as a sage.

title: Elder Son of Jambugāmika: Are you satisfied with the robe you wear? Are you delighted with adornments? This fragrance emanates from your virtue, Not from…
content:
Elder Son of Jambugāmika:

Are you satisfied with the robe you wear?
Are you delighted with adornments?
This fragrance emanates from your virtue,
Not from others producing such a scent!

title: - From how many things, Venerable Sir, does the Tathāgata dwell withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind? - From ten things, Bāhuna, does the Tathāgata…
content:
- From how many things, Venerable Sir, does the Tathāgata dwell withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind?
- From ten things, Bāhuna, does the Tathāgata dwell withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind. What ten? From form, Bāhuna, the Tathāgata dwells withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind. From feeling, Bāhuna... From perception, Bāhuna... From formations, Bāhuna... From consciousness, Bāhuna... From birth, Bāhuna... From aging, Bāhuna... From death, Bāhuna... From this suffering, Bāhuna... From defilements, Bāhuna, the Tathāgata dwells withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind. Just as, Bāhuna, a blue lotus, or a red lotus, or a white lotus, born in the water, grown up in the water, rises up above the water and stands without being stained by the water. So too, Bāhuna, from these ten things, the Tathāgata dwells withdrawn, detached, liberated, with unlimited mind.

title: One who knows the passing away and rebirth of beings everywhere, Not attached, well-gone, Awakened, enlightened — Him I call a brahmin. One whose destination…
content:
One who knows the passing away and rebirth of beings everywhere,
Not attached, well-gone,
Awakened, enlightened —
Him I call a brahmin.

One whose destination is not known by devas, gandhabbas, or humans,
A taintless one, an Arahant —
Him I call a brahmin.

One who has nothing in the past, future, or present,
Who has nothing, who clings to nothing —
Him I call a brahmin.

The bull, the excellent, the hero,
The great sage, the conqueror,
The passion-free, the purified, the awakened —
Him I call a brahmin.

One who knows his former lives,
Who sees heaven and hell,
Who has reached the end of birth,
Who has attained direct knowledge, perfected,
The sage, the seer,
Perfect in all accomplishments —
Him I call a brahmin.
